MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SPX COOLING TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: J. Bruce Jackson, Esq. of Keller, Fishback & Jackson LLP 775-1206 for pltf & opposing party; David J. Mann, Esq. of Vasquez Estrada & Conway LLP 453-0555 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant SPX's Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden as to causation, and Plaintiffs failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether Decedent's asbestos exposure is attributable to Defendant. In light of the Court's ruling as to Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment, Defendant SPX's Motion for Summary Adjudication as to Plaintiffs' causes of action for False Representation and Intentional Tort and claim for punitive damages is moot. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Angie Diner, CSR 581 (415/748-4030)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer Ways of PRINDLE, AMARO, GOETZ, HILLYARD, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), appearing for defendant / moving party University Mechanical & Engineering Contractors, Inc
J. Bruce Jackson of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Leslie Morris, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT UNIVERSITY MECHANICAL & ENGINEERING CONTRACTORS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ALTERNATIVELY SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant University Mechanical & Engineering Contractors, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication (strict liability, premises owner/contractor liability, false representation and intentional tort causes of action) are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence (1) that Ms. Morris was exposed to asbestos-containing products or material attributable to defendant and that such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to her asbestos-related illness, (2) that defendant breached a duty owed to Ms. Morris, or (3) that all of plaintiff's claims against defendant are barred by Workers Compensation exclusivity. By failing to submit with its moving papers verified factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his claims against defendant, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff's objections on the grounds of defendant's failure to submit verifications are sustained. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Defendant also failed to submit evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claims against defendant. As to summary adjudication, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his causes of action for strict liability, premises owner/contractor liability, false representation and intentional tort. By failing to submit with its moving papers verified factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support these claims, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff's objections on the grounds of defendant's failure to submit verifications are sustained. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Defendant also failed to submit evidence to negate an essential element of any of these causes of action. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Novak's declaration are sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 11596 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Nov-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Hans C. Ruschke of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
J. Bruce Jackson of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Leslie Morris, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. By failing to submit verified factually devoid responses with its moving papers, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff objected on the grounds of no verifications. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Defendant failed to sustain its burden under the negation of an element prong of Aguilar because defendant did not submit admissible evidence negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Shelley K. Tinkoff of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party NIBCO, Inc.
J. Bruce Jackson of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Leslie Morris, et al.


RULING:
DEFENDANT NIBCO, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant NIBCO, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of issues 1, 2 and 3 (false representation, intentional tort and punitive damages) are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Ms. Morris' exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to her asbestos-related disease. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Specifically, plaintiff identified himself as a person with knowledge supporting his claim for negligence, defendant's special interrogatories did not ask what knowledge he had and defendant failed to demonstrate that Mr. Garrett does not have knowledge supporting his claim for negligence. As to summary adjudication of issues 1, 2 and 3, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his false representation and intentional tort causes of action and claim for punitive damages. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Specifically, plaintiff identified himself as a person with knowledge supporting these claims, defendant's special interrogatories did not ask what knowledge he had and defendant failed to demonstrate that Mr. Garrett does not have knowledge supporting his false representation and intentional tort causes of action and claim for punitive damages. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION
Off Calendar Jul-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S DEMURRER TO INTERVENORS' COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION.
Appearances: Timothy D. F. Barteau of Selman Breitman LLP (979.0400) for intervenors and responding party; Cassiana Aaronson of Keller Fishback & Jackson LLP (818.342.7442) for plaintiff and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's general demurrer based on timeliness is overruled. Plaintiff's demurrer to affirmative defenses 7-8, 11-14, 17-19, 22-23, 25-27, 29-35, 38, 40-41 for failing to specifically refer to any causes of action is overruled. Even if they did not explicitly refer to a certain cause of action, the affirmative defenses at issue are stated in a manner by which they may be intelligently distinguished. Plaintiff's demurrer to affirmative defenses 7-14, 17-19, 22, 23, 27, 30, 31, 35, 38, 41 for failure to state facts sufficient to constitute defenses and for uncertainty is overruled. An answer must aver facts as carefully and with as much detail as the facts which constitute the cause of action and which are alleged in the complaint. (FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384.) Given the lack of factual specificity of the allegations in the first amended complaint against Intervenors' insured, Scott Co. of California, it is not reasonable to expect Intervenors to plead affirmative defenses with greater factual specificity. The proof of service submitted with Intervenors' opposition, as well as the court's review of Lexis, demonstrate that Intervenors timely filed and served their opposition. Order signed. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Not reported. (503/TLJ)

Off Calendar Mar-29-2011 Text Ruling

Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California¿S Demurrer To Plaintiff¿S Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-10-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Wes Wagnon, Esq. of KELLER FISHBACK & JACKSON, LLP, appearing for the Plaintiffs
Elizabeth R. Bain, Esq. of FOLEY & MANSFIELD, LLP, appearing for defendant UC Regents

RULING:
Morris v. Alta Building Material 275685
After argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:
Defendant The Regents of the University of California's demurrer demurrer to plaintiffs' fifth and sixth causes of action (premises owner/contractor liability and loss of consortium) is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Defendant is a public entity, as defined by Government Code section 811.2, and is immune from liability under Government Code section 815. Government Code section 835 provides a statutory basis for imposing liability on a public entity. Plaintiffs' complaint fails to allege the statutory basis under which Defendant may be held liable, and facts that meet the requirements of Government Code section 835. The sixth cause of action for loss of consortium is derivative of the Fifth Cause of Action. On that basis, the demurrer to the sixth cause of action is also sustained with 10 days leave to amend.

Defendant The Regents of the University of California's motion to strike punitive damages from plaintiffs' complaint is granted. Defendant is a public entity, as defined by Government Code section 811.2. A public entity is not liable for punitive damages. Government Code 818.

Ms. Bain to prepare & submit a proposed order.

Judge Marla J. Miller, Clerk C. Banayad, Reporter L. Najera, CSR 5153
